messages.content:
China’s largest banks cut rates for the nation’s $453 billion corporate US dollar deposits for the second time in a matter of weeks, according to people familiar with the matter, as authorities intensify measures to shore up the struggling yuan. At least nine banks, including the four big state lenders, removed the spread they previously offered over the US Secured Overnight Financing Rate for corporate clients, the people said, declining to be identified discussing private information. Before the latest reduction, banks were offering 5.7% for one-year deposits, down from 6% about a month ago. The SOFR stands at 5.09%.

summarize in less than 30 words and give me a title

China's largest banks have cut rates for corporate US dollar deposits for the second time in weeks, as authorities aim to strengthen the struggling yuan. The spread previously offered over the US Secured Overnight Financing Rate has been removed by at least nine banks, including the big state lenders. The rate for one-year deposits has decreased from 6% to 5.7%, with the SOFR standing at 5.09%.

Title: Chinese Banks Slash Rates for Corporate US Dollar Deposits Amid Efforts to Stabilize Yuan

Alibaba Gets Home-Province Backing for AI, Digital Economy Plans
China’s tech giant Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. aims to work with its home province, one of the nation’s wealthiest, to develop artificial intelligence and the digital economy.

Zhejiang, a province on China’s eastern seaboard, entered a cooperation agreement with Alibaba, vowing to create a better business environment for platform-economy firms and to encourage innovations in areas including artificial intelligence, according to a report of Securities Times on Wednesday.

While the backing for Alibaba came from a provincial, not national, government, a separate statement Wednesday showed that a national regulator backed Zhejiang in taking the lead in trials for platform-economy innovation. That deal was signed between the State Administration for Market Regulation and Zhejiang.

The developments suggest official backing for Alibaba’s efforts, against a backdrop in recent years of regulatory moves against the firm. Chinese policymakers have also been steadily calling for innovative advances in digital applications, as part of a broader effort to support economic growth.

Alibaba Chief Executive Officer Daniel Zhang, who signed the pact with local government officials, said that the digital economy has entered a new age with artificial intelligence sweeping the world this year, according to the report.

summarize in less than 20 words and give me a title

China's tech giant Alibaba has received backing from its home province of Zhejiang for its AI and digital economy plans. 

Title: Alibaba Receives Support from Home Province for AI and Digital Economy Initiatives
summarize in less than 50 words

Alibaba Group Holding has secured support from its home province, Zhejiang, to develop artificial intelligence (AI) and the digital economy. The cooperation agreement aims to create a better business environment for platform-economy firms and encourage innovation in areas including AI.

Meta launches Instagram Threads in a direct challenge to Twitter

Threads is a text-based messaging app that looks very similar to Twitter.
People will be able to use their Instagram usernames on Threads and follow the same accounts on Threads as they do on Meta’s photo-sharing app.
The release of Threads is noteworthy considering that Twitter has suffered a wave of mishaps under the ownership of Tesla CEO Elon Musk, leaving the popular social messaging app vulnerable to competing apps.

summarize with 50 words

Meta has launched Instagram Threads, a text-based messaging app that closely resembles Twitter. Users can use their Instagram usernames on Threads and follow the same accounts as they do on Instagram. This move poses a direct challenge to Twitter, which has recently faced various issues under Elon Musk's ownership.

Quick Take: (1) FOMC minutes wrap-up (2) Powell signals more rate hikes (3) Chinese banks cut USD deposit rate
Federal Reserve Divisions Revealed in June Meeting Minutes

The Federal Reserve officials showed divisions in their decision to pause interest-rate increases at their June meeting, with some participants favoring a quarter-point increase. However, the minutes indicated that the majority of policymakers agreed that more tightening would likely be needed later in the year. The readout suggests that the committee remains divided on the pace and intensity of policy moves to combat inflation. 
Powell Signals Fed Open to Two Straight Hikes, but Divisions Remain 
Despite previously claiming a unanimous decision, Chair Jerome Powell has acknowledged that most officials see the need for at least two more rate increases this year. Jerome Powell’s messaging has influenced market expectations, narrowing the gap between officials’ projections and market expectations. However, some Fed officials have expressed caution and believe there is time to wait and observe the impact of current policies. Concerns about stubborn inflation and the need for more work on interest rates to achieve the 2% inflation goal remain prevalent among policymakers.


translate into traditional chinese

美聯儲六月會議記要顯示內部分歧

美聯儲官員在六月份的會議中對停止利率上升的決定表現出分歧，一些與會者傾向於加息0.25個百分點。然而，記要指出大多數政策制定者一致認為，往後可能需要進一步收緊政策。這表明該委員會對於抗擊通脹的政策步伐和力度仍存在分歧。

鮑威爾表示美聯儲開放連續兩次加息，但分歧仍然存在
盡管先前宣稱決策是一致的，鮑威爾主席承認大多數官員認為今年還需要至少兩次加息。鮑威爾的表述影響了市場的預期，使官方預測與市場預期之間的差距縮小。然而，一些美聯儲官員表達出謹慎態度，認為有時間等待觀察當前政策的影響。對於頑固的通脹和實現2%通脹目標所需要的進一步工作，政策制定者仍然普遍存在擔憂。

Quick Take: (1) FOMC minutes wrap-up (2) Powell signals more rate hikes (3) Chinese banks cut USD deposit rate

translate into traditional chinese

簡要說明：(1) FOMC 會議紀要總結 (2) 鮑威爾暗示將進一步加息 (3) 中國銀行削減美元存款利率

FOMC紀要會議揭示六月份美聯儲官員在停止加息的決定上存在分歧，其中部分與會成員傾向於加息0.25個百分點。不過紀要中顯示，大部分政策制定者同意後續可能需要進一步收緊政策。這表明該委員會在應對通脹問題的政策步伐和力度上存在分歧。

鮑威爾則暗示多數官員認為今年還需要進行至少兩次加息。鮑威爾的表態影響了市場的預期，縮小了官方預測和市場預期之間的差距。然而，一些美聯儲官員則表示謹慎，認為應該等待並觀察當前政策的影響。對於續採取措施應對棘手的通脹問題和進一步為實現2%的通脹目標所需工作，政策制定者仍然存在相當大的關切。

中國銀行則削減了美元存款利率。

Chinese Banks Slash Rates for Corporate US Dollar Deposits Amid Efforts to Stabilize Yuan
China’s largest banks have cut rates for corporate US dollar deposits for the second time in weeks, as authorities aim to strengthen the struggling yuan. The spread previously offered over the US Secured Overnight Financing Rate has been removed by at least nine banks, including the big state lenders. The rate for one-year deposits has decreased from 6% to 5.7%, with the SOFR standing at 5.09%.

Other news:
Alibaba Receives Support from Home Province for AI and Digital Economy: Initiatives Alibaba Group Holding has secured support from its home province, Zhejiang, to develop artificial intelligence (AI) and the digital economy. The cooperation agreement aims to create a better business environment for platform-economy firms and encourage innovation in areas including AI. 

Bank of America said on Wednesday it planned to increase its quarterly common stock dividend to 24 cents per share from 22 cents per share beginning in the third quarter of 2023.
Meta has launched Instagram Threads, a text-based messaging app that closely resembles Twitter. Users can use their Instagram usernames on Threads and follow the same accounts as they do on Instagram. 

translate into traditional chinese

中國銀行為了穩定瀕臨困境的人民幣，再次削減了公司美元存款利率。這些價格調整是繼前幾週後的第二次調整，目前至少有九家銀行，包括大型國有銀行，取消了此前超過美國隔夜拆借利率的利差。一年期存款利率已從6%下降到5.7%，美國隔夜拆借利率為5.09%。

其他消息：阿里巴巴獲得家鄉浙江省對人工智能和數字經濟計劃的支持。合作協議旨在為平台經濟公司創造更好的商業環境，並鼓勵在包括人工智能在內的領域進行創新。

美國銀行周三宣布計劃從2023年第三季度開始，將每股普通股股息從22美分提高到24美分。

Meta推出了Instagram Threads，這是一款非常類似Twitter的基於文字的消息應用程序。用戶可以在Threads上使用自己的Instagram用戶名，並關注與他們在Instagram上相同的帳戶。

Chinese Banks Slash Rates for Corporate US Dollar Deposits Amid Efforts to Stabilize Yuan

translate into traditional chinese

在努力穩定人民幣的措施下，中國最大的銀行削減了企業美元存款的利率。

Note: "Corporate US Dollar Deposits" can be translated as "企業美元存款" or "公司美元存款". Both translations are commonly used in traditional Chinese.

more impactful

PBOC sets red line to defend yuan / Premier Li vows swift economic recovery measures

another version

Leading EV manufacturers sign pact to ensure fair competition in China

Tesla, along with 16 top electric-vehicle makers in China such as BYD, Nio, Xpeng, Geely, and Chery, have signed an agreement ( 《汽車行業維護公平市場秩序承諾書》)  to maintain fair competition and avoid abnormal pricing in the Chinese EV market.

write me a title within 6 words , catchy
EV Giants Unite for Fair Competition
